svg.text: KMP vs Z vs Boyer-Moore vs Rabin-Karp
Naive O(1) O(n·m) worst short patterns, simple code
KMP O(m) O(n+m) worst-case linearity, online streams
Z-algorithm O(m) O(n+m) same as KMP, simpler proofs
Boyer-Moore O(m + σ) O(n/m) sublinear large alphabets, long patterns
Rabin-Karp O(m) O(n+m) avg multi-pattern (set of patterns)
Aho-Corasick O(Σ|P_i|) O(n + matches) many patterns simultaneously
Z[i] = length of longest substring starting at i that matches a prefix of S.
Built with a "Z-box" sliding window in O(|S|). Search by computing Z on P + "$" + T:
any Z[i] ≥ |P| with i > |P| reveals a match in T.
Same O(n + m) bound as KMP, conceptually simpler proofs.
KMP wins for streaming text (process one char at a time without buffering).
Z-algorithm wins for offline pattern problems and contests.
Aho-Corasick generalizes KMP to a trie of multiple patterns.
